danger_signals.visual_form: Screen darkens, day/night cycle visual shift
danger_signals.meaning: Enemies become stronger, more aggressive, deal increased damage; villagers replaced by hostile zombies

danger_signals.visual_form: Stronger enemy sprites appear in same locations
danger_signals.meaning: Nighttime enemies are more dangerous variants of daytime counterparts; visual distinction indicates threat level change
Cannot access safe services (merchants, information); environmental corruption indicates danger zone

danger_signals.visual_form: Distinct mansion tileset with ornate, decrepit visuals
danger_signals.meaning: Danger area containing puzzle challenges and hostile enemy variants (e.g., mansion-specific 'Franken' enemy)

safety_signals.visual_form: Bright screen palette, day cycle visual
safety_signals.meaning: Weaker enemies; safer to explore and gather resources (hearts)

safety_signals.visual_form: Villager sprites present in town areas
safety_signals.meaning: Safe zone; can access merchants, receive information, obtain items and clues

safety_signals.visual_form: NPC merchant sprites in specific locations
safety_signals.meaning: Safe transaction point; can purchase whips, items, magical weapons for progression

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Whip weapon, holy items (Holy Water, Magic Cross)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Simon fights standard enemies and boss-variant enemy types
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Villages where NPCs aid Simon; mansions where Dracula's body parts are hidden

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Dracula's body parts (Rib, Eye, Heart, etc.); undead/zombie visual theme
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Daytime zombies (weaker), nighttime zombies (stronger); mansion-specific 'Franken' variant; boss encounters
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Five mansions as strongholds; Dracula's castle ruins as final location; graveyards

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Replaced by zombies at night (corrupted state)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Villages throughout the game world; only accessible during daytime

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Bright, lighter screen palette; visible NPCs in villages; weaker enemy sprites present
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies are less aggressive and deal reduced damage; villagers offer dialogue (though often misleading); merchants available for transactions; safer exploration window

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Dark, dimmed screen palette; zombies replace villagers in towns; stronger enemy sprite variants appear
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies gain strength and increased damage output; villagers unavailable; merchants inaccessible; higher heart drops from defeated enemies; player must navigate hostile environment

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Game mechanics reflect time pressure (cycling day/night); multiple possible endings based on completion speed
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must complete objective within time limit (8 game days for best ending); slower completion results in fatal injury endings; curse mechanically manifests as temporal constraint

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Inventory items representing five body parts (Rib, Eye, Heart, Nail, Ring) plus Magic Cross
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Collected parts provide gameplay benefits (e.g., Dracula's Rib blocks projectiles); final collection allows passage to Dracula's castle blockade removal

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Distinct mansion tileset distinct from village/outdoor environments; ornate, decrepit aesthetic
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Contains puzzle-solving requirements; houses specific enemy types; guards Dracula body parts as progression gates

environmental_storytelling.example: Daytime shows functioning village with living NPCs; nighttime shows same locations corrupted by undead, visually communicating Dracula's supernatural influence over the land
Villagers provide cryptic or false clues about item locations and progression; game design choice embeds unreliability into world narrative—civilization is compromised by curse

environmental_storytelling.technique: Enemy type variation by location and time
environmental_storytelling.example: Same outdoor areas contain weak zombies during day, strong variants at night; visual escalation communicates danger progression and encourages strategic timing of exploration
Five distinct mansions house Dracula's body parts; architectural distinctness from villages visually marks them as dangerous, corrupted sanctums requiring specific items to traverse
Collecting five body parts and Magic Cross creates visual inventory manifestation; symbolic reassembly of Dracula communicates Simon's burden and the desperate nature of curse-breaking

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Weaker attack patterns; lower damage output; drop fewer hearts on defeat
Stronger attack patterns; increased damage; higher heart drops; visually distinct sprite variant
Hostile; prevents NPC interaction; indicates location corruption; implies villager transformation or death
Concept art indicates: walks toward player; causes ground-shaking effect; involves collapse mechanic—more dangerous than outdoor variants
Must collect specific items to progress; time-constrained completion (8 days for best outcome); uses whip and magical weapons; gains experience/power through heart collection
Offers dialogue (often misleading); some provide actual clues; available only before nightfall; replaced by zombies at dusk
Sells items, whips, magical weapons; accepts hearts as currency; only transacts during daylight hours; inaccessible at night

notes: Castlevania II introduces world-state visualization primarily through day/night cycle mechanics. Danger signals are communicated via palette shifts, enemy sprite swapping, and NPC replacement. The game's nonlinear open-world design requires visual distinction between safe (villages, daytime) and dangerous (outdoors at night, mansions) zones. Faction identity is implicit rather than explicit: Simon opposes Dracula's undead forces, which manifest visually through zombie enemies and corrupted locations. The curse mechanic is communicated through time pressure rather than visual affliction. Environmental storytelling relies heavily on NPC dialogue (intentionally unreliable) and location-based visual design. Unused content suggests originally planned sixth Dracula body part (fangs) and mansion-specific enemy variant, indicating design iteration in danger signal development.
